title: Firm Standard — Data Processing & Commercial Terms (v4)
text:
FIRM STANDARD TERMS (v4)

1. Liability. Aggregate liability is capped at 100% of the annual fees payable under the agreement. Caps below 1x annual fees require partner approval and a recorded rationale.

2. Governing law. Agreements are governed by the laws of England & Wales unless a different governing law is approved by the supervising partner.

3. International data transfers. Transfers of personal data outside the UK/EEA must rely on Standard Contractual Clauses together with a documented transfer risk assessment and supplementary measures, consistent with Schrems II.

4. Confidentiality. Mutual confidentiality obligations survive for three years after termination.

clauses.liability: Aggregate liability capped at 100% of annual fees; sub-1x caps require partner approval.
clauses.governing_law: Governed by the laws of England & Wales unless the partner approves otherwise.
clauses.data_transfer: Non-UK/EEA transfers require SCCs plus a transfer risk assessment and supplementary measures (Schrems II).
clauses.confidentiality: Mutual confidentiality survives three years post-termination.

title: Process Document — Document Review Severity Policy
text: Severity is set up front by policy, not inferred by a model after the fact. Task types and their severity labels are defined here.

review_binding_obligation.label: Review of a binding obligation (liability, indemnity, data transfer)

review_governing_law.label: Review of governing law / jurisdiction clause

review_recital_summary.label: Summary of a non-key recital / background

title: Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
text: Article 44–49 set the conditions for transfers of personal data to third countries: such transfers require appropriate safeguards. Article 17 establishes the right to erasure.

supported_claims: transfers of personal data to third countries require appropriate safeguards
data subjects have a right to erasure

title: Case C-311/18 — Data Protection Commissioner v Facebook Ireland and Schrems (Schrems II)
text: The Court invalidated the EU-US Privacy Shield and held that controllers relying on Standard Contractual Clauses must verify, on a case-by-case basis, whether the law of the third country ensures adequate protection and must adopt supplementary measures where it does not.

supported_claims: the EU-US Privacy Shield is invalid
transfers relying on SCCs to the US require supplementary measures
controllers must assess third-country law before transferring personal data

title: Regulation (EC) 593/2008 — Law applicable to contractual obligations (Rome I)
text: Article 3 provides that a contract is governed by the law chosen by the parties. Article 4 provides default rules where no choice is made.

supported_claims: parties may choose the law governing their contract
absent a choice of law, default connecting factors determine the governing law

title: Directive (EU) 2019/790 — Copyright in the Digital Single Market
text: Articles 3 and 4 provide exceptions for text and data mining. The Directive concerns copyright; it does not address international personal-data transfers.

supported_claims: research organisations benefit from a text and data mining exception

title: Draft — Data Processing Agreement, Project Atlas
text:
3.2 Liability. The aggregate liability of each party is capped at 50% of the fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim.

7.1 International transfers. Personal data may be transferred to the supplier's US affiliates; the parties agree such transfers are permissible without additional safeguards.

9. Governing law. This agreement is governed by the laws of the State of New York.

clauses.liability: Aggregate liability capped at 50% of trailing-12-month fees.
clauses.data_transfer: Transfers to US affiliates permitted without additional safeguards.
clauses.governing_law: Governed by the laws of the State of New York.

title: Draft — Governing Law & Jurisdiction clause, Project Atlas
text: 11. Governing law and jurisdiction. The parties may select the governing law of this agreement. The courts of England & Wales have exclusive jurisdiction.

clauses.governing_law: Parties may select the governing law; English courts have exclusive jurisdiction.

title: Draft — Background recital summary, Project Atlas
text: Recital A. The parties have collaborated since 2021 and wish to record the commercial background to this agreement. No operative obligations arise from this recital.

title: Draft — Confidentiality clause, Project Atlas
text: 14. Confidentiality. Each party shall keep the other's confidential information secret. These obligations survive for three years after termination.

clauses.confidentiality: Mutual confidentiality surviving three years post-termination.
